dialogos.texto_en: This is that Shurijo Castle we went to earlier.
Ah! There's also a picture of Umikaji Terrace!
I understand how the artist feels.\nThe scenery of this place makes you want to draw it.
Yeah, I took a photo at the same spot.
Nino and Miku, eh.\nWhat are you looking at?
Ah, it's you two.
Oh, we're looking at hand-drawn postcards.\nLook, they have illustrations of Okinawa's scenery.
Wow...\nThe illustrations are really detailed.
It seems like a local artist drew them.\nIt's because they're familiar with the scenery.
Yes, all of them evoke the scenery of each place.
What about this big bridge...\nThe name's written, but how do you read it?
Oh, that's Kouri Bridge.
You know your stuff.
I wanted to visit Kouri Island,\nso I did some research. Well, it doesn't seem\nlike we'll have the chance this time.
I heard it's a famous scenic spot in Okinawa,\nwith a bridge that spans over the sea.\nI can understand why just by looking at this picture.
What about this one... Is it the Blue Cave?
I've heard of that too. Isn't it a famous diving spot?
This forest-like illustration over here is beautiful too.
I wonder where it is%p-2;――
There really are so many famous places in Okinawa.\nWith so many views I've never seen before.
But I don't think everything in Okinawa\nis illustrated in these postcards.
If we want to fully enjoy Okinawa,\nwe'll need more than just a few days.
Maybe we should have taken a longer trip.\nThen we could have gone to more places.
Well, why don't we rent a villa\nand stay for about a month?\nThat would be a great way to fully enjoy Okinawa.
That's a great idea.\nIt would be the ultimate trip to enjoy Okinawa.
(Renting a villa for a month...\n That's a trip I can't even imagine as a commoner...)
Th-that's not good, is it?!
Huh? Why?
I think it'd be fun.
B-because! Living under one roof with a man...\nthat's not appropriate, is it?
WHAAA?!
Oh, I see what you mean.
You've lived on a deserted island with Futaro before,\nso it's too late to be worried about that now.
Ooh...\nWell, that's true...
Besides, you're blushing over something like this?
Huh?
In the end, you're going to live with Fu-kun, right?
Ah...
L-living together!
What are you getting all red for?\nDon't imagine weird things.
I-I-I, I wasn't imagining anything weird!\nI was just imagining normal things!
What exactly do you mean by 'normal'?
(But, well, as Nino said...\n that day might come eventually.)
Even so, it's hard to imagine\nsomething so far off in the future.
Imagining it is certainly difficult, but%p-2;――
Well, eventually.\nThat time might come too.
You're not denying it.
There's no need to deny it, right?\nIf you think about the future.
Y-yeah...
Even so, it's hard to imagine\nsomething so far off in the future.
Imagining it is certainly difficult, but%p-2;――
Living together, like a family...
A f-family?!
I mean, Itsuki, um...\nYou did say you were considering marriage, right?
Yeah, she did say that.
F-family...
...Itsuki?
She's frozen.
H-hey, are you alright?
WHAAA!!
E-excuse me, I'm alright.\nI'm back to my senses.
Yes, some day when I actually live with you, Uesu%p-2;――\n%p;Futaro...
Even so, it's hard to imagine\nsomething so far off in the future.
Imagining it is certainly difficult, but%p-2;――
I'm sure it will be a fun life together.\nNo, I'll definitely make it happen!
Y-yeah, I think so too.
(I can't imagine what it will be like,\n but as Itsuki said, it's definitely going to be fun.)
W-what do you think?\nWhy not start thinking about furniture now?\nLike what kind of sofa we want!
I think it's a bit too early for that...\nBut as long as it's comfortable to sit on,\nI don't really mind.
Isn't that too broad of a range?!
...It's becoming their own little space, isn't it.
Geez.\nI was just trying to tease them a little,\nand they had to go and show me up like this.
But, as I mentioned earlier,\nit's surprising how proactive Itsuki has become.
I feel like she's changed since coming to Okinawa.\nI don't know why, though.
In any case, I want her to consider\nthe timing and situation.
